body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** commit 067f84057594a8976c95ad2138138ce139ae1130 (PR #57985) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed The initial beta release (`1.0.0-beta.1`) of `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` was published on 2026-04-09. The package provides declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, exposing resource types including `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer` or any child resources (databases, firewall rules, etc.) - **Authenticate the Client section** has the heading but no content — should explain that deployment is done via `azd` and point to provisioning docs - **Next steps section is empty** — no links to related packages, REST API docs, or Azure portal - **Key concepts section lacks resource descriptions** — lists no individual resource types or their purpose; the library exposes 11+ provisionable resource classes with no guidance on which to use - **No sample test files exist** — `tests/` contains only a `.csproj`; there are no `Samples/` files with snippet-backed examples that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject into the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Fill in the empty/incomplete sections: **Authenticate the Client section** — replace the empty section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client This library uses the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) to deploy the declarative infrastructure you define. There is no runtime client authentication required — provisioning resources is done via `azd up` or similar commands. For more information see the [Azure Developer CLI documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/). ``` **Key concepts section** — replace the current one-liner with: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. Key resource types provided: - **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — The primary resource representing an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server. Configure SKU, version, storage, backup, high availability, and network settings. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — A database hosted on a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — A firewall rule controlling network access to a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — A server-level configuration parameter. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — An Azure AD administrator account for a Flexible Server. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** — Represents a server backup. - **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Scheduled maintenance window configuration. - **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Threat protection settings for a Flexible Server. ``` **Examples section** — replace the empty section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server with a Database and Firewall Rule ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ``` **Next steps section** — replace the empty section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/mysql/flexible-server/overview) - Learn about the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/) - Explore related provisioning packages: [`Azure.Provisioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Provisioning), [`Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateBasicMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { AdministratorLogin = "mysqladmin", Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ads_v5", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new("mysqlDb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infra.Add(database); MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infra.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } } ``` **Snippet details:** - Snippet name: `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` - Test file path: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` - README location: under `## Examples`, the placeholder `` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic `` followed by closing `` ``` `` ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current state of `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Heading only, no content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ One-liner, no resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ Empty | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects the `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` snippet into `README.md`; verify the git diff shows the snippet was inserted correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only if any public types or members were added 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [MySql] Docs: Add examples and complete README for Azure.Provisioning.MySql initial beta release
